short_description.value: RingMo-Aerial is the first large foundation model specifically tailored for Aerial Remote Sensing (ARS) vision tasks. It introduces Frequency-Enhanced Multi-Head Self-Attention (FE-MSA), an affine transformation-based contrastive learning pre-training method, and the ARS-Adapter for efficient fine-tuning. The model achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple ARS and ApRS datasets and tasks, including semantic segmentation, object detection, scene classification, change detection, 3D reconstruction, and object tracking.

pretext_training_type.value: Masked Image Modeling (MIM) + Contrastive Learning (CL) with affine transformations

masking_strategy.value: Random masking (SimMIM) for MIM; affine transformation-based positive/negative pairs for CL

pretraining.value: Two-stage pretraining: Stage 1 uses Masked Image Modeling (SimMIM) to capture pixel-level semantic associations; Stage 2 uses contrastive learning with affine transformations to learn ARS-specific visual features.

dataset.value: Large-scale composite of open-source airborne remote sensing datasets (urban, rural, wilderness, multi-season, multi-perspective)

application.value: Urban scene segmentation from UAV imagery
